subject: Re: Warp template matching
body:
Hi Chen,

Please try out https://github.com/warpem/warp-tm-vis, running it should be
as easy as uvx warp-tm-vis once you've installed uv
<https://docs.astral.sh/uv/getting-started/installation/>.
Features:
- visualize superposed tomogram + correlation volume in 2D & 3D
- visualize particle positions from template matching star files
- simulate the results of filtering at a given cc threshold
[image: image.png]

Cheers,

Alister

On Mon, 10 Feb 2025 at 13:28, 'Chen Sun' via Warp <warp-em@googlegroups.com>
wrote:

> Hello,
>
> I am doing template matching with Warptools in command line. It generated
> output files successfully. However, I don't know if they are picking on the
> right particles. Is there a way for me to visually inspect the matched
> particles and manually adjust them?
>
> Best regards,
> Chen
>
